notes: Attack-shape profiles (Phase 0 audit §6). These are resolution-policy metadata, not packet schemas and not token geometry.
Area attacks: roll below 10 auto-misses, hits take full damage, misses take half, natural 20 hits all targets but does not double damage, cover can negate miss damage.
Verified = derived from SWSE core rules, not from pack heuristics.

profiles.name: Autofire (2x2 area)

sourceRefs.note: Autofire targets a 2x2 area, applies the autofire attack penalty, and consumes 10 shots.
Area attacks compare one attack roll against each target, deal full damage on hits and half damage on misses, and do not double damage on a natural 20.

notes: -5 attack penalty (-2 braced autofire-only), consumes 10 shots. Matches combat-actions.db 'Autofire' ruleData.

profiles.name: Burst Fire (feat, single target)

sourceRefs.note: Burst Fire uses an autofire-capable weapon against one target rather than resolving an area attack.

notes: Single target, -5 attack, +2 weapon dice, 5 shots. NOT an area attack — Evasion does not apply.

profiles.name: Grenade / explosive burst

sourceRefs.note: Burst-radius attacks are centered on a grid intersection and resolved as area attacks.
Grenades and explosives determine the center of the burst radius at a square corner before the attack roll.

notes: Default burst radius 1 square; individual grenades may override radius.

profiles.name: Splash weapon

sourceRefs.note: Splash weapons resolve the primary target separately from adjacent targets.
Primary targets take full damage on a hit and half damage on a miss; adjacent targets take half damage only if hit.

notes: Primary target full/half; adjacent targets compare vs. their own defense.

profiles.name: Cone area attack

sourceRefs.note: Cone attacks are area attacks; source-specific data determines the size.

notes: Delivery stays with the owning source (weapon or force-power); size set per source.

profiles.name: Line area attack

sourceRefs.note: Line attacks are area attacks; source-specific data determines the size.

notes: Delivery stays with the owning source (weapon or force-power); size set per source.
